intro: Ronnbergia drakeana, also known as Dragon's Blood, is a plant-based medicine derived from the latex of the Croton lechleri tree, native to the Amazon rainforest. The latex has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues, wounds, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and protective properties. The bioactive constituents of Ronnbergia drakeana include anthraquinones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Ronnbergia drakeana include capsules, tablets, and topical creams, which are used to support digestive health, promote wound healing, and reduce inflammation.
